reasoning: I will summarize the common symptoms of bulimia from the provided text, organizing them into behavioral, physical, and emotional/psychological signs, with note of typical weight patterns and complications.
output:
- Behavioral symptoms
  - Recurrent binge eating episodes with a sense of loss of control
  - Eating large amounts of food in secret
  - Purging after binges (vomiting, laxatives, enemas, or diuretics)
  - Excessive or compulsive exercise
  - Going to the bathroom right after meals
  - Spending a lot of time exercising
  - Feelings of disgust, guilt, or fear after binges
  - Weight commonly normal, but individuals may perceive themselves as overweight

- Physical symptoms (observed or detectable)
  - Dental problems: cavities, gum infections, enamel erosion or pitting
  - Dry mouth
  - Swollen or pouch-like cheeks
  - Broken blood vessels in the eyes
  - Calluses or small cuts on the tops of the finger joints from vomiting
  - Electrolyte imbalance or dehydration (low potassium possible)

- Psychological signs
  - Preoccupation with body weight and shape
  - Self-perception of being overweight despite normal weight or fluctuations
  - Feelings of guilt or shame related to eating

- Potential medical complications (less common but serious)
  - Esophageal tears or irritation
  - Pancreatic problems
  - Heart rhythm issues from electrolyte disturbances
